name: Bhai Gursharan Singh (Damdami Taksal)
body: Bhai Gursharan Singh and Bhai Gurmail Singh, both renowned for their Kirtan performances, arrived in the UK after the tragic events of Operation Blue Star in 1984, which saw the attack on Sri Harmandir Sahib, Amritsar. At the time of the attack, they were actively performing Kirtan, and the profound impact of those events led to their emigration around 1986. They were accompanied by Bhai Amrik Singh who played the tabla with them after arriving to UK. However, soon after their arrival, Bhai Amrik Singh got married and left the ‘jatha’ (group).

In the UK, Prof. Amar Singh, who was responsible for organizing programs at Guru Har Rai Gurdwara in West Bromwich, Birmingham, became instrumental in bringing Bhai Gursharan Singh and Bhai Gurmail Singh to various Kirtan events and Darbars. Prof. Amar Singh also sought the aid of Sardar Jasvindar Singh Sagoo to book them for performances at R.S.T. Graham Street in Birmingham, as well as other Gurdwaras across the region. As a member of their ‘jatha’, Prof. Amar Singh contributed his expertise as a tabla player and occasionally accompanied them on the harmonium.

Bhai Gursharan Singh Ji’s journey into Gurbani and Kirtan began at a young age. He studied under the guidance of esteemed scholars at Damdami Taksal in Mehta Chowk, where he received formal education in Gurbani Santhiya (pronunciation and recitation) and Kirtan. He also earned a degree from the Mobile University run by Damdami Taksal. Bhai Gursharan Singh was closely mentored by Sant Kartar Singh ‘Bhindrawale’, gaining invaluable spiritual knowledge. Raised by devoted Gursikh parents, he developed a deep connection with Gurbani from an early age. Over the years, Bhai Gursharan Singh Ji performed Kirtan at Sri Darbar Sahib in Amritsar and traveled internationally, spreading the message of Gurbani through his soulful renditions across Canada, the USA, and Europe. He also has a website dedicated to Gurbani Santhiya, helping others learn and gain a better understanding of Gurbani. His son, Hari Singh, has followed in his footsteps and occasionally joins him in his Kirtan performances.

Sadly, Prof. Amar Singh, who was also a prominent music teacher in Birmingham, passed away four years ago. Prof. Amar Singh founded his own music academy in Birmingham and was deeply involved in the preservation and promotion of Kirtan. He played a key role as a trustee for the Harvallabh Sangeet Festival, an annual music festival held in Jalandhar, and was appointed as the Principal for the UK division of Pracheen Kala Kendra, Chandigarh. Through his dedication, he taught hundreds of students in the UK, many of whom successfully completed external exams for Pracheen Kala Kendra.
